title: LowMesh™ Pocket-Ranger | Portable Mesh Tracker Node

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: The Pocket-Ranger is a compact, ruggedized and injection molded mesh networking device engineered for secure, off-grid communication. Built on the powerful RAK4630 module, it provides a seamless bridge between your smartphone and a decentralized LoRa network. Whether you are hiking deep into the backcountry, preparing for emergency scenarios, or operating in areas with zero cellular infrastructure, the Pocket-Ranger ensures you stay connected. It enables end-to-end encrypted text messaging and location sharing across a resilient local mesh network. Key Features Dual Platform Compatibility: Fully compatible with Meshtastic® and MESHCORE™ software ecosystems. Use the web flasher located at lowmesh.com. High-Performance Hardware: Powered by the RAK4630 module (nRF52840 + SX1262) @ 3.1V for superior range and efficiency. Extended Battery Life: Integrated 2000mAh LiPo battery with NTC protection, providing up to 4 weeks of runtime (usage dependent) with implementing 50% RX Duty Cycle. Versatile Controls: Features a dedicated User Button, Reset Button, and Power Switch for tactile operation. Plug & Play Updates: USB-C port for both high-speed charging and easy firmware flashing (USB-C to USB-A cable included). External Antenna: Optimized for maximum signal penetration and range with a +3dBi gain. Pocket-Ranger is available at https://shop.lowmesh.com/products/pocket-ranger

embeds_html:
Pocket-Ranger
The Pocket-Ranger is a compatible Meshtastic and MeshCore networking device designed for secure, off-grid communication. Perfect for outdoor adventures and more
messages.reactions_html: 4

messages.author: Skye
messages.text: Injection molded.... music to my ears
messages.content_html: Injection molded.... music to my ears

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: All the holes are covered, the speaker buzzer uses an IP67 eptfe membrane, a USB-C waterproof connector, a silicon cover internally that protect water ingress from coming in near the USB and button area and another custom silicon gasket at the seam of the 2 plastic parts. I dont have any IP rating yet but its pretty weatherproof.
messages.content_html: All the holes are covered, the speaker buzzer uses an IP67 eptfe membrane, a USB-C waterproof connector, a silicon cover internally that protect water ingress from coming in near the USB and button area and another custom silicon gasket at the seam of the 2 plastic parts. I dont have any IP rating yet but its pretty weatherproof.
messages.reactions_html: 2
messages.reply_html:
Skye
How weather resistant is it?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: I’ll review the Canada shipping when I get home. It comes with Meshtastic but I have my own web flasher on my website that allows you to easily switch between the 2 platforms. The strap clip and holster are not available yet. The strap clip should be ready less than a week and the holster in about 2 weeks or so

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Any chance there's an internal antenna version coming out?
messages.content_html: Any chance there's an internal antenna version coming out?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: There is! I have some prototype PCBs being made right now and the mold was made so I can have 2 different version, 1 with internal and 1 with external. The internal antenna version comes in 2 prices, $49.99 and $69.99 for the PRO model. PRO will have RTC, GNSS, BME280 sensor and a few other tweaks.

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: This should probably be added to the page - it emphasises the fact it's suitable for outdoor adventures and is ruggedized but doesn't clarify how it protects against moisture ingress, which would be a big drawcard for those who might want to consider this over a Tag or T1000E

messages.reply_html:
bluebrolly.
All the holes are covered, the speaker buzzer uses an IP67 eptfe membrane, a USB-C waterproof connector, a silicon cover internally that protect water ingress from coming in near the USB and button area and another custom silicon gasket at the seam of the 2 plastic parts. I dont have any IP rating yet but its pretty weatherproof.

messages.author: PANiCnz
messages.content_html: Is international shipping available?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: That and it has a USB-C port instead of that magnetic cable people seem to be losing! Just wait until the GPS version comes out, peoples heads are gonna spin when they see how long the battery life is. The GPS version uses the backup mode of a special quectel GNSS module and only draws about 16uA in sleep mode and has its own super cap backup battery to hold the last satellites, time and other metadata so it can acquire faster locations when it wakes up again. It only draws about 10mA in operational mode.
